channel.title: bas_ — dispatches
channel.description: Long-form notes on systems, software, and things worth measuring.

item.title: The Lever Goes Backwards
item.description: Interventions in complex systems often invert themselves. From colonial rat bounties to industrial quotas, the cobra effect demonstrates why incentives reorganize behavior in unexpected ways.

item.title: When Formulas Freeze
item.description: Complexity theory meets statistical physics. Analyzing the sharp phase transitions where Boolean satisfiability problems transform from trivial to exponential.

item.title: Entropy to Order: How I Rebuilt My Second Brain in Mordor
item.description: I had a thousand bookmarks and zero ideas. This is the long story of how I tore the whole thing down, named the new system Mordor, and started writing notes that actually argue with me.

item.title: The Mass and the Mirror
item.description: A deep dive into the lineage of crowd psychology—from Le Bon's warnings to Bernays' manuals—and the danger of the exempted vantage point.

item.title: The Lying Layer
item.description: Every layer of abstraction in a modern computer is a useful fiction. Learning to see through these fictions separates code writing from system understanding.
